title: tragopogon porrifolius subsp. sativus
intro: Tragopogon porrifolius subsp. sativus, also known as salsify or veal root, is a subspecies of the perennial plant Tragopogon porrifolius that is cultivated for its edible root. The root of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of salsify root include its potential to support digestive health, lower blood pressure, and aid in weight management, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Salsify root can be prepared in various herbal forms, including dried root, tincture, and decoction, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine.
